images.caption: Figure 1:Performance of𝑽0.5\boldsymbol{V}_{\text{{0.5}}}across six diverse mathematical reasoning benchmarks, demonstrates superiority over GRPO(Shaoet al.,2024)and DAPO(Yuet al.,2025), achieving faster convergence and some over 10% performance improvement.

Figure 2:Demonstration of PPO, GRPO, and our proposedV0.5V_{0.5}framework.While PPO requires a synchronously trained value model and GRPO relies on the empirical group mean,V0.5V_{0.5}computes an adaptive baseline by fusing a prior from a frozen generalist value model (V0V_{0}) with sparse empirical rollouts via a dynamic weightww(detailed inTheorem 3.3,Equation 6, andEquation 7).

Figure 3:Evolution of policy gradient norm.V0.5V_{0.5}maintains a lower and more stable gradient norm than GRPO. By trading a strictly bounded bias for a reduced baseline MSE, it effectively neutralizes the variance amplification inherent in sparse rollouts.

Figure 4:Evolution of policy entropy.While GRPO’s high-variance gradients cause rapid entropy decay,V0.5V_{0.5}leverages low-noise baseline estimation to sustain higher entropy, ensuring robust exploration in reasoning tasks.

Figure 5:Performance ofV0.5V_{0.5}under extreme sparsity(1,2,4,1,2,4,and88rollouts)vs.standard GRPO (1616rollouts). To ensure a fair comparison, prompt batch sizes are adjusted to maintain constant per-step computational overhead.
